Well, thanks for being here.
We appreciate your time.
Mark, please give us a brief bio on yourself.
My name is Mark.
I was born in 1991.
I was born in Memphis, Tennessee, but I was raised in a smaller country town.
It's Middleton, Tennessee.
It's about 60 miles, I'd say, east of Memphis.
And we just got a red light, a Dollar General, about two gas stations, and a hardware store, and that's about it.

Yeah, and it's not even a red light either.
It's just a blinking light.
You just pull up, it's got a four-way, and you just ease on through.

But I just prefer the country myself.
Well, that makes two of us.
I don't blame you there.
Mark, around 2008, when you were around 16 years old, a friend of yours told you about something he saw that really shook him up.
What more can you tell us about that?
So he was, his name's Sam, and we've been best friends.
First time we ever met was actually on the school bus.
We rode the same school bus, bus 26.
And believe it or not, we got into a fight the first time we met.
And we were arguing, and I said, he dared me to hit him.
And I took a pin out of my pocket, and I drew an X on his face, and I punched him square in the face.
Well, of course, we had to go to the principal and everything.
And once we were on the way to the principal's office, they just so happened called our names over the intercom.
And we met.
We said, hey, when we go in the office, let's pretend like we're friends and that we did this on purpose.
So we did that.
We went into the office and we told the principal that we were just playing.
Well, just so happened after all that, we just became best friends and we played baseball together as well.
So we're pretty close-knit, and we met probably fourth grade, I would say.
And like I said, we just became best buds, and we had a small group of people.
Everybody that rode Busch 26, we all hung out outside of school as well.
Sam lived on kind of like the western part of the county, and then I lived on the southern part,
which is only about 10 miles from each other.
But he lived in a community called Roger Springs, and I lived down by the state line.
And like I said, we used to always ride bicycles
and go out and have a good time like kids would do.

Well Sam one night nobody was around so he got out on his bike and he was going down Foster Road and it a big old long hill and then there a railroad track and you could kind of jump the track if you get

and like you could see everything so that is one reason he was going riding too down the road as
you could see well he come down that hill and he jumped the tracks and he slammed on his brakes and
and he slid sideways, and there's kind of a gravel parking lot where a dump's at right there.

There would be about four dumpsters set up,

and they would throw stuff beside the cans and stuff like that.

but it gave him a big scare.
